description:
You are a health informatics specialist and VistA system analyst at a VA medical center. Your medical director has requested a comprehensive clinical data completeness audit to identify the single patient with the richest, most complete clinical record across all major VistA subsystems — this patient's record will be used as a reference case for staff training on VistA data navigation.

Background:
VistA is a federated system with data distributed across independent subsystems for different clinical domains. A truly complete patient record will have data in the pharmacy subsystem, the patient vitals subsystem, the allergy documentation subsystem, the problem list subsystem, and the patient demographics subsystem. Identifying the patient with the most comprehensive cross-system data requires systematic analysis of all four clinical data domains.

Objective:
Find the single patient with the highest combined data volume across all four clinical domains (pharmacy + vitals + allergies + problems) using the following composite scoring formula:
  composite = (prescription_count * 3) + (vital_count) + (allergy_count * 2) + (problem_count * 2)

Identify the patient with the highest composite score and compile their complete clinical profile.

Deliverable:
Create the file /home/ga/Desktop/patient_profile.txt containing:
1. Header: "COMPREHENSIVE PATIENT PROFILE - VistA Data Completeness Report"
2. Patient identification: full name, patient identifier, date of birth, sex
3. Pharmacy section: total outpatient prescription count
4. Vitals section: total vital sign recording count
5. Allergies section: total allergy entry count
6. Problems section: total problem list entry count
7. Composite data completeness score (using the formula above)
8. A narrative clinical summary (3+ sentences) discussing the patient's overall clinical complexity

Success Criteria:
- File /home/ga/Desktop/patient_profile.txt exists and was created during this task session
- File identifies the correct highest-composite-score patient by name
- File contains data from at least 3 of the 4 clinical domains
- File includes a numeric composite score or individual domain counts
- File contains a clinical narrative

metadata.expected_outcome: File /home/ga/Desktop/patient_profile.txt created during the task session, containing the name and clinical data profile of the patient with the highest composite data completeness score across all four clinical domains.
